matrix.trace: MaxgravityApp.swift -> MGRootView -> bootstrap() -> bridge.fetchConnectionStatus() -> HTTP /v1/connection/health -> response status -> UI state update (shows MainShellView or FirstLaunchView)
matrix.notes: Verified. If paired, redirects to MainShellView. If not, shows FirstLaunchView.

matrix.trace: MGFirstLaunchView -> presentedSheet = .pairingCode -> MGPairingCodeSheet -> QR scan or manual entry -> pair() -> bridge.pairDevice() -> HTTP POST /v1/connection/trust -> trustStore.save() -> Keychain save
matrix.notes: The UI setup and REST API calls are fully coded, but actual Keychain persistence and camera Scanning on physical hardware is not fully verified end-to-end (PARTIAL).

matrix.trace: MGPairingCodeSheet -> ScannerViewController -> checkCameraPermissions() -> AVCaptureDevice.authorizationStatus(for: .video) -> requests permission if notDetermined -> setupCaptureSession()
matrix.notes: Uses standard AVFoundation AVCaptureDevice API. Works in native iOS target, but simulator defaults to fallback.

matrix.trace: MGCameraScannerView -> Coordinator -> metadataOutput() -> scannerDidFindCode() -> onScan() callback
matrix.notes: Coded natively in MGCameraScanner.swift. Untested on physical device.

matrix.trace: decodePairingPayload() -> JSONDecoder.decode(MGPairingQRCodePayload.self) -> returns struct or attempts URL components percent decoding
matrix.notes: Fully implemented with multiple fallback paths (raw JSON, Base64 query parameter, percent encoded query parameter).

matrix.trace: PairingManager.trustDevice() -> record.expiresAt.getTime() <= now.getTime() -> throws PAIRING_EXPIRED
matrix.notes: Implemented and verified by vitest suite (tests/pairing.test.ts).

matrix.trace: MGPairingCodeSheet -> confirmationView -> shows payload.bridgeFingerprint -> user manual confirmation
matrix.notes: The fingerprint is merely shown in confirmationView; there is no cryptographic verification of the bridge public key/fingerprint during trust negotiation (MOCK).

matrix.trace: PairingManager.trustDevice() -> Consumes session -> returns deviceSecret -> TrustStore.save()
matrix.notes: Coded on bridge. However, there is no desktop-side UI prompt to explicitly approve the trust confirmation before issuing credentials; the bridge trusts immediately once pairing token matches.

matrix.trace: MGKeychainHelper.save() -> SecItemAdd() -> Keychain storage
matrix.notes: Implemented in iOS using SecItemAdd. Not verified on physical device.

matrix.trace: MGAppModel.startTaskEventStreaming() -> URLSession.webSocketTask() -> WS /v1/tasks/:taskId/events -> Server authenticates header -> returns event stream
matrix.notes: Coded in MGServices.swift and server.ts. Bypasses SSL cert verification using MGTrustAllCertsDelegate.

matrix.trace: MGAppModel.webSocketTask.receive() failure callback -> no automatic reconnect retry loop
matrix.notes: Reconnect behavior has no retry loop or offline queues modeled in Swift (SCAFFOLDED).

matrix.trace: MGSettingsScreenView -> Disconnect -> disconnectCurrentComputer() -> bridge.disconnect() -> KeychainHelper.delete() / Revoke REST API call /v1/connection/trusted-devices/:deviceId/revoke
matrix.notes: Implemented in UI and fully verified in Vitest suite.

matrix.trace: MGSpacesView -> appModel.spaces -> bootstrap() -> bridge.listSpaces() -> HTTP /v1/spaces -> maps roots to spaces
matrix.notes: Implemented. Maps approved workspace roots on the PC directly to Spaces in the app.

matrix.notes: Implemented. Conversations are loaded from the bridge's local tasks.json database.

matrix.notes: Fully implemented. Spawns new Antigravity process on the Windows machine.

matrix.notes: Scaffolded. The selection UI exists, but the permission mode is not sent in the bridge request or enforced by the bridge shell during execution.

matrix.notes: Implemented. Runs validation against the language server.

matrix.notes: Spawns the local Antigravity CLI process in the workspace CWD.

matrix.trace: child.stdout.on('data') -> aggregates stdout text -> parses final JSON payload on exit
matrix.notes: There is no live token-by-token stdout stream to the client. The bridge waits for the process to exit, then parses the final response (DEAD_CODE).
